description: A query that fetches by id without a tenant predicate lets a caller read another tenant's record by guessing or iterating ids.
body: Failure mode: a handler loads a resource by id alone, assuming the id belongs to the caller's tenant, so changing the id reaches another tenant's data. Detection signals: the query has no tenant predicate; a request as tenant A returns tenant B's row by id; there is no negative cross-tenant test. Do not trust that an id implies tenant ownership. Safer fallback: derive the tenant from the authenticated principal, add a tenant predicate at the data layer, back it with row-level security, and add a negative test that a foreign tenant is denied; escalate as a high-severity leak.
